abstract:
To proactively address challenges and new developments in its rapidly changing task
environment, ICRISAT is mapping out a new Strategic Plan to 2020. This initiative is also
being undertaken to pursue the recommendation of ICRISAT’s sixth External Program and
Management Review (EPMR) in 2009-2010.
Essentially, strategic planning enables us to:
institution: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
publisher: International Crops Research Institute for the Semi-Arid Tropics
note:
Many scientists and staff members contributed to this paper that provides background
information and analysis for the forthcoming strategic planning effort. I thank David
Hoisington and Cynthia Bantilan for establishing comprehensive terms of reference.
Further discussions with Dave were useful in focusing the work from ICRISAT’s perspective.
V Aruna and B Hanumanth Rao ably furnished administrative assistance in a very friendly
working environment. I am particularly grateful to P Parthasarathy Rao and Pratap Singh
Birthal who contributed data sets and exchanged ideas that laid the foundation for this
report. Indeed, Pratap’s and Partha’s knowledge of suitable data is one of the strengths of
the report.
Inputs from Peter Cooper, K Palaniswami, R Padmaja, Farid Waliyar, Pierre C Sibiry Traore,
K.PC Rao, Kamanda Josey Ondieki, P Anand Babu, Mike Butterfield, and Jupiter Ndjeunga
were important in shaping the work. Wasim Ahmed, E Jagadeesh, SK Chandrakala, and
Shraavya Bhagavatula skillfully provided research support. Members of ICRISAT’s
Management Committee commented on a first draft of the report.
title: Challenges and Opportunities for Agricultural R&D in the Semi-Arid Tropics
